reforms.description: Portugal has access to Community structural funds aimed at promoting infrastructural investment, human capital formation, and private physical capital formation, which includes support for innovation and modernization expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding supports innovation and modernization, which are expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively utilized since its implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in funding for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: EC membership has also brought with it access to Community structural funds, for which the entire country is eligible.

reforms.description: Portugal increased training expenditures under the Community Support Framework, aiming to enhance human capital and improve educational outcomes.
reforms.package_name: Community Support Framework for Human Capital
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in training and education is expected to improve the skills of the workforce, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively utilized since its implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in training expenditures is significant but does not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Human capital has been enhanced by increasing the school-leaving age by three years in 1986 and by rapid increases in training expenditures under the Community Support Framework (near $1 billion per year).
